title: Dog Skin Peeling in Sheets: Causes & Emergency Triage Guide
meta_desc: If your dog's skin is peeling in sheets, it is a high-priority emergency. Learn about potential causes like TEN or burns and why to see a vet now.
content_html:
What Causes Skin to Peel in Large Sheets?

When a dog's skin peels away in large sheets or layers, it is medically referred to as sloughing. This is a severe symptom often associated with deep tissue damage or systemic illness. Common causes include Stevens-Johnson Syndrome (SJS), Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis (TEN), severe thermal or chemical burns, or extreme vasculitis where the blood supply to the skin is cut off. These conditions cause the top layers of the skin to die and separate from the underlying tissue.

Is This a Medical Emergency?

Yes, the urgency level for skin peeling in sheets is High. This is a critical situation that requires immediate veterinary intervention. Because the skin acts as the body's primary barrier, losing large sections puts your dog at an extreme risk for secondary infections, sepsis, severe dehydration, and inability to regulate body temperature. Furthermore, these conditions are incredibly painful and require professional pain management and supportive care.

Why Taking a Photo is Crucial for Triage

If you are in transit or waiting for a callback, taking a clear photo of the affected areas can be lifesaving. A photograph helps the triage team evaluate the distribution of the lesions and the severity of the tissue loss. It also provides a baseline for the veterinarian to determine if the sloughing is spreading rapidly. This visual information allows the hospital to prepare a sterile environment and specific treatments before your arrival.

faqs.question: Can I apply over-the-counter ointments to the raw skin?
faqs.answer: No. Do not apply any creams, ointments, or home remedies. These can contaminate the area, trap heat, or cause further irritation to the exposed dermis. Keep the area covered only with a clean, dry, non-stick cloth if necessary during transport.

faqs.question: What are common symptoms that accompany skin sloughing?
faqs.answer: Dogs may also exhibit high fever, extreme lethargy, loss of appetite, and visible pain. You may see redness, blistering, or 'slipping' skin (Nikolsky sign) in other areas of the body.

faqs.question: Could this be an allergic reaction?
faqs.answer: Yes, some of the most common causes of skin peeling in sheets are severe adverse drug reactions or an overactive immune response. Your vet will need a full history of any recent medications or vaccinations.
